meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve no coding experience (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no coding experience. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup for Non-Coders (Beginner Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop guessing and start scaling. Learn to implement professional analytics without coding using Antigravity and Claude Code in this founder-focused guide.
content.answer_block: You don't need a CS degree to master your metrics. By leveraging Antigravity for seamless integration and Claude Code for automated implementation, you can deploy enterprise-grade analytics in minutes. This approach transforms raw user behavior into actionable revenue signals, allowing you to iterate faster and validate your SaaS product's market fit.
content.intro:
The silent killer of most SaaS startups isn't a lack of features; it's the blind spot created by missing data. You are pouring energy into building, but without instrumentation, you are flying a plane in a thunderstorm without a radar. Every user drop-off is a mystery, and every conversion is a fluke you cannot replicate.

But here is the secret: you don't need a dedicated data engineering team to see the truth. Modern AI tools have democratized technical implementation. By treating your codebase like a puzzle that AI can solve, you can bridge the technical gap between your vision and your metrics, turning your product into a high-performance growth engine.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Identify the three events that signal user success. We focus on 'Sign-up,' 'Feature Engagement,' and 'Successful Payment' to keep the implementation lean.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a growth engineer. Create a schema definition for a SaaS analytics tracking plan focusing on sign-ups, feature usage, and subscription events. Output in JSON format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a tracking_events.json file based on the provided schema definition to standardize our event logging.'

steps.title: Inject Analytics Middleware
steps.description: Create a centralized event logger so you don't have to manually code analytics into every single component.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a clean, reusable TypeScript middleware function that captures events defined in our schema and pushes them to a generic analytics provider endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the middleware in /src/middleware/analytics.ts and integrate it into the main application router.'

steps.title: Instrument the Conversion Funnel
steps.description: Map your UI actions to the tracking events defined in step 1 to ensure you capture the user journey accurately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a code snippet to trigger a 'button_click' event with dynamic properties for the signup CTA in my React component.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Scan my components and automatically add the analytics trigger to all primary CTA buttons.'

steps.title: Validate Event Streaming
steps.description: Verify that your data is actually flowing from the client to your analytics dashboard before you launch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test script that simulates user interactions and verifies that the analytics middleware sends the correct payload to the console.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run the test script to validate that all events are correctly formatted and transmitted.'

steps.title: Automate Dashboard Reporting
steps.description: Connect your analytics data to a visualization tool to ensure you can see your growth metrics in real-time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a configuration guide to sync my event logs with a lightweight dashboard tool like PostHog or Mixpanel using an API key.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure the environment variables and initialization logic to connect our event stream to the production dashboard.'

pro_tips: Always track the 'Time to First Value'—it is the single most important metric for reducing churn in early-stage SaaS.
Avoid 'tracking fatigue' by only measuring events that directly correlate with your subscription revenue.
Use Claude Code to perform 'automated code audits' every week to ensure new features haven't broken your analytics pipeline.
Implement server-side tracking for payment events to prevent ad-blockers from skewing your MRR data.
Create a 'Data Dictionary' in your README so you never lose track of what each event property represents as your app scales.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, spend 48 hours observing user behavior. Identify the point of maximum drop-off and use Claude Code to refactor that specific user flow to increase conversion.
content.conversion_block: Stop guessing what your users want and start building what they pay for. With this foundation, you have the visibility to scale your revenue and master your product's growth. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
